metadata.description: A curated collection of skills for AI coding agents.

plugins.name: document-search
plugins.description: Search a folder for a term and return a ranked list of files that contain it — including inside .docx, .pptx, .xlsx, and .pdf documents where ordinary grep/rg are blind.

plugins.name: document-readers
plugins.description: Read text content from a single PDF, Word, Excel, or PowerPoint document. Four companion skills (pdf-reader, docx-reader, xlsx-reader, pptx-reader) — install together for full Office/PDF reading support.

plugins.name: knowledge-tools
plugins.description: Maintain a personal, LLM-curated knowledge base (wiki). The llm-wiki skill packages Karpathy's LLM-wiki method as a schema + workflows + helper scripts that let an agent consistently maintain a structured, long-lived notes folder.

plugins.name: document-organizer
plugins.description: Safely organize a folder of files — classify by content, group by mtime/extension, find duplicates, or batch-rename. Four modes, one skill, with a scan → plan → execute → undo pipeline that never mutates anything without dry-run review.

plugins.name: document-inspector
plugins.description: Structural inspection of office documents — returns the deterministic facts an LLM cannot derive from extracted text. Three sibling skills, one per file type: pdf-inspector (metadata + AcroForm fields), docx-inspector (metadata + tracked changes + heading structure), xlsx-inspector (metadata + formula dependency graph + named ranges). Companion to the document-readers plugin — readers give you the text, inspectors give you the structure around it.

plugins.name: code-tools
plugins.description: Tools for understanding and working with codebases. Currently ships code-inspector — stdlib-only, read-only, gitignore-aware folder map with two feature tiers: overview (languages, entry points, frameworks, test layout) and ast (per-file Python AST analysis: classes, top-level functions, imports, statement count, max nesting depth, most-complex-files ranking). Planned additions: non-Python AST via tree-sitter and a code-review skill for diff + linter orchestration.
